title: Consolidated Data Model
description: Consolidated entity schemas from data model files

catalog_system_abbreviation.description: Stable abbreviation for this catalog (primary key; e.g. Sc, Mi, Facit).

name.description: Full name of the catalog or reference work.

notes.description: Edition notes, regional scope, or usage caveats.

catalog_system.description: Reference system used for catalog numbers on individual stamps; one row per system you track. Primary key is the conventional abbreviation (Mi, Sc, …).

country_id.description: Stable identifier; prefer ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 when practical, otherwise a short mnemonic slug (e.g. dk, ddr).

display_name.description: Human-readable country or postal administration name.

notes.description: Optional clarifications (colonies, period splits, obsolete administrations).

ebay_category.description: Optional eBay.com (EBAY_US taxonomy) stamp leaf category id for Browse ``category_ids`` when searching or filtering listings for this country.

issue_series.description: Optional nested stamp sets or series for this country (pairs with issue_series.country_id, parent_array issue_series).

scott_catalog_entries.description: Optional nested catalog listings for this country (pairs with scott.country_id on scott_catalog_entry, parent_array scott_catalog_entries).

numeral_cancels.description: Optional nested numeral killer / obliterator-to-town mappings (pairs with numeral_cancel.country_id, parent_array numeral_cancels).

country.description: Normalized territory for cataloging, filtering, and joins; identifiers should remain stable across inventory and listings.

function_name.description: Function name in the provider API.

parameters.description: Parameters in declaration order (nested under each function in source data; omitted on flattened parent rows).

output.description: Maps each response property to the anchor parameter whose composite_key builds that object.

response_key.description: Property name on the function result.

anchor_parameter.description: Parameter name with composite_key for that assembly.

gemini_function_call.description: One row per tool exposed to Gemini (see xframe/data/gemini_function_calls.json).

name.description: Parameter name in the tool schema.

schema_entity.description: Consolidated $defs entity used for JSON Schema typing.

composite_key.description: Maps PK components on schema_entity to sibling parameter names.

required.description: Whether the parameter is required on the tool.

enum.description: If true, restrict to enum from consolidated data where applicable.

gemini_function_parameter.description: Declaration field for a single parameter. Primary key is function_name plus parameter name so flattened rows are globally unique.

round_index.description: Zero-based order of this completion within the invocation.

total_tokens.description: Gemini totalTokenCount / OpenRouter total when present.

thoughts_tokens.description: Gemini thoughtsTokenCount when thinking models report it.

cached_content_tokens.description: Gemini cachedContentTokenCount when context caching applies.

tool_use_tokens.description: Gemini toolUsePromptTokenCount when tool-use billing is split out.

invocation_token_round.description: Snake_case fields align with Gemini usageMetadata and OpenRouter usage. round_index is 0 for the first completion, then increments per follow-up (e.g. each tool round). tool_use_tokens maps Gemini toolUsePromptTokenCount.

usage_anchor.description: Fixed discriminator for this summary object; appraisal writes aggregate.

by_round.description: Usage per completed provider HTTP response.

totals.description: Sum of numeric fields across by_round (missing keys treated as 0).

invocation_token_usage.description: Embedded under invocation_event_file.token_usage. usage_anchor is always the literal aggregate for this per-file summary object.

requested_model.description: Model id from the invocation config (Gemini model id or OpenRouter route); must match llm_model.model_id when that catalog row exists.

actual_model.description: Upstream model when it differs from requested_model (e.g. OpenRouter free pool); omitted when identical or unknown. When set, should match llm_model.model_id for the serving route.

out.description: Returned listing text length when status is ok; otherwise 0.

char_counts.description: UTF-16 code unit counts: user prompt in; model completion text out (0 on error or empty body).

turn_count.description: Successful provider completions (each returned model message) during the run.

function_calls.description: Tool names in invocation order (may repeat); each value should match gemini_function_call.function_name when that declaration exists.

token_usage.description: Present only when at least one usage round was captured.

error_message.description: Present when status is error and a message was captured.

output_text_chars.description: Present when status is ok: UTF-16 length of returned listing text (0 when the model returned empty text).

invocation_event_file.description: Root object of each event file. Omit optional keys when not applicable: actual_model only when it differs from requested_model; token_usage only when at least one usage round was recorded; error_message only on status error with a message; output_text_chars only on status ok.

series_id.description: Stable unique id for this series within the catalog.

country_id.description: Primary issuing country or administration for this series.

name.description: Display name of the set or series (e.g. 1875 bicolors, Europa 1980).

description.description: Scope, catalog cross-references, or how stamps are grouped in this series.

year_from.description: First year of issue represented in this series, if applicable.

year_to.description: Last year of issue for multi-year series; omit for single-year or ongoing.

parent_series_id.description: Optional parent series for nested grouping (e.g. country definitives under a broad program).

issue_series.description: Groups stamps into sets, definitive series, commemoratives, or specialized series for browsing and pricing context.

listing_id.description: Surrogate primary key for this listing row.

target_price.description: Guide or asking price as a decimal in listing_currency.

listing_currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code for target_price (closed list; extend enum as needed).

listing.description: Separate from philatelic attributes on stamp; omit when no price is tracked or use multiple rows for history or multi-channel asks.

model_id.description: Stable id passed to the provider API (e.g. models/gemini-2.0-flash or OpenRouter route slug).

name.description: Human-readable model name for UI and logs.

provider_id.description: Upstream provider that serves this model.

has_free_tier.description: Whether the vendor documents a no-cost or trial quota for this model (informational only).

rate_limits.description: Operator notes: max RPM/TPM/RPD (or usage-dashboard cap), vendor doc links, token pricing pointers; empty if unknown.

feedback_events.description: Optional curated operator notes per catalog model. Not written by appraisal; use xframe/data/invocation_stats.json for automated invocation telemetry.

llm_model.description: Rows describe which vendor model id to pass to the client (e.g. gemini-2.0-flash, openai/gpt-4o). has_free_tier and rate_limits are indicative documentation for operators; they are not enforced by this app. feedback_events is optional and separate from invocation_stats.json.

feedback_event_id.description: Stable unique id for this event (e.g. UUID, or a deterministic hash of at_iso + invocation_id + message_preview prefix).

prompt_id.description: Prompt template id for the appraisal run (matches invocation_stats.events[].prompt_id and xframe/data/prompts/<prompt_id>.json).

actual_model_id.description: Vendor model id that served or failed (matches invocation_stats.events[].model).

requested_model_id.description: Invocation or router model when it differs from actual_model_id (matches invocation_stats.events[].requested_model). Omit when identical.

in.description: User prompt string length in UTF-16 code units.

out.description: Model reply text length in UTF-16 code units; 0 when the attempt failed before any body.

char_counts.description: UTF-16 lengths of user prompt text sent (in) and model completion returned (out); matches invocation_stats.events[].char_counts.

turn_count.description: Successful provider/model completions before failure (matches invocation_stats.events[].turn_count).

at_iso.description: When the event was observed (ISO-8601 timestamp).

invocation_id.description: Appraisal prompt_invocation.invocation_id when the event came from a run (e.g. numeral_cancel_identification_openrouter_free).

reason_code.description: Normalized reason derived from the upstream message (matches appraisal rate-limit bucketing).

message_preview.description: Truncated error or body text for operators (keep short; full payloads may live in CI logs).

llm_feedback_event.description: Aligns with invocation_stats.json event shape: actual_model_id, requested_model_id, char_counts, turn_count, function_calls.

country_id.description: Issuing country or postal administration for this numeral assignment.

numeral.description: Integer shown in the numeral cancel or killer; strictly less than 999.

period_start.description: First date (inclusive) for which this mapping is valid.

numeral_cancel_key.description: Composite primary key (country, numeral, period start).

country_id.description: Issuing country (must match numeral_cancel_key.country_id; scalar FK enables nesting under country.numeral_cancels during consolidation).

period_end.description: Last date (inclusive) for which this mapping is valid; must be on or after period_start in the key.

town_name.description: Town, city, or post office name corresponding to the numeral for the given period.

history.description: Optional provenance or reassignment notes (e.g. territorial changes, postal renumbering).

numeral_cancel.description: Each row ties one integer numeral from a postal cancel (1–998) to a town or office name for a given country; the primary key is country, numeral, and period start—period_end is the inclusive end of the validity window when resolving a strike date.

prompt_id.description: Stable id matching the filename stem (e.g. stamp_listing, xframe); referenced by upload commit metadata prompt field.

brief.description: Short label for UI or docs.

preferred_model.description: Default Gemini model id when this prompt has no prompt_invocations entries (or none enabled), or when GEMINI_MODEL overrides only the fallback path.

output_format.description: Whether appraisal writes listings/<stem>.txt (plain model text) or listings/<stem>.json (parsed catalog object).

template_lines.description: Prompt body lines; appraisal joins with newline. Placeholders: __FIRST_LINE__, __META_JSON__, __TARGET_JSON__, __NOTES_JSON__. For output_format json, Gemini and OpenRouter use API structured output (stamp array schema), not inlined consolidated schema.

gemini_output_lines.description: Optional lines appended to template_lines only for google_gemini when output_format is txt. Omitted for json prompts (structured output is set in the API client). Same placeholders as template_lines.

prompt_invocations.description: Optional nested appraisal targets (provider + model) for this prompt. When omitted or empty, appraisal uses a single Gemini call with preferred_model. Exactly one enabled row should have primary_for_listing true when any are present.

prompt.description: One row per prompt id; template_lines are joined with newlines for the model. Files live under xframe/data/prompts/<prompt_id>.json in entity-store shape { "prompt": [ { ... optional prompt_invocations: [...] } ] }.

invocation_id.description: Unique id (used in sidecar filenames); e.g. stamp_listing_openrouter.

prompt_id.description: Prompt template this invocation belongs to. Omit in nested source rows under prompt.prompt_invocations (implied by parent).

provider_id.description: Provider row (provider_id selects the appraisal client; see provider entity).

model.description: Model id for this provider (Gemini model id or OpenRouter model slug, e.g. openai/gpt-4o-mini).

sort_order.description: Stable ordering for logs and merge; lower runs first in listings.

primary_for_listing.description: If true, this invocation's output is the main listings/<stem> file the uploader polls.

enabled.description: If false, appraisal skips this invocation. When omitted, treated as true.

prompt_invocation.description: Nested under prompt.prompt_invocations in xframe/data/prompts/<prompt_id>.json. All enabled invocations for a prompt run in parallel. Set enabled false to skip a row without deleting it. Exactly one enabled row per prompt should have primary_for_listing true; that output is listings/<stem>.{txt|json}. Others are listings/<stem>__<invocation_id>.{txt|json}. If none are enabled, appraisal falls back to a single Gemini call using prompt.preferred_model.

provider_id.description: Provider key; appraisal maps each to a client (Gemini SDK vs OpenRouter HTTP).

api_base_url.description: REST API base URL (no trailing path after version segment where applicable). Gemini: v1beta base used by Google AI; OpenRouter: https://openrouter.ai/api/v1

notes.description: Env vars, headers, or auth notes.

provider.description: Rows live in xframe/data/providers.json. Appraisal selects the client from provider_id (google_gemini → GEMINI_API_KEY + @google/genai; openrouter → OPENROUTER_API_KEY + chat/completions with vision).

country_id.description: Issuing country or postal administration for this listing (denormalized; use with country.scott_catalog_entries nesting).

section.description: Scott major-section letter or pair (e.g. B, RW); omit for regular postage.

variation.description: Optional plate or variety letter suffix (closed list).

issue_series_id.description: Stamp set or series this catalog listing belongs to, when known.

issue_date.description: Required issue date for this catalog listing (ISO 8601 date string).

denomination.description: Required face value denomination as printed or cataloged (for example 5c, 10c, 1¢, $1).

perforation.description: Perforation as given for this listing (e.g. perf 11½, comb 13¾, imperforate, roulette).

primary_color.description: Dominant or catalog-listed ink or paper color for this listing (e.g. carmine, blue, bister).

description.description: Required short listing description (design subject, issue context, or key distinguishing detail).

notes.description: Variety, plate, or supplement commentary for this listing.

scott_catalog_entry.description: Identifies a stamp design or major listing in a catalog system (Scott, Michel, etc.). Primary key is the composite field scott (country_id, section, number, variation). Optional issue_series_id ties the listing to a set or series row; optional perforation records the catalog-listed gauge or style. A stamp instance optionally points here via the same composite key; additional cross-references stay in stamp_catalog_number.

section_prefix.description: Scott catalog number prefix as printed before the numeral (e.g. B, C, QE, RW).

description.description: What stamps in this section represent and how they differ from regular postage.

sort_order.description: Optional display order in reference lists (lower first).

scott_catalog_section.description: Each Scott-listed stamp number begins with a section prefix (e.g. B for semi-postal, RW for hunting permits). One row per prefix; use for filtering, UI labels, and matching catalog numbers.

stamp_id.description: Unique id (SKU, upload basename, or UUID).

country_id.description: Issuing country or postal administration for this listing (denormalized; use with country.scott_catalog_entries nesting).

section.description: Scott major-section letter or pair (e.g. B, RW); omit for regular postage.

variation.description: Optional plate or variety letter suffix (closed list).

scott.description: Optional primary catalog listing for this stamp (same composite key as scott_catalog_entry.scott). Cross-catalog lines remain on stamp_catalog_number.

title.description: Short title or working label (often aligned with listing title).

summary.description: Identification notes, variety callouts, or catalog commentary.

issue_year.description: Primary year of issue for this stamp or single-stamp lot.

issue_year_to.description: End year for multi-year issues or sets represented by one row.

condition_quality.description: Overall condition or gum state for mint/used grading.

type.description: Cancel or postmark class (e.g. circular datestamp, numeral killer, machine, slogan).

city.description: Town or post office name read from the cancel, when present.

numeral.description: Numeral or barred obliterator value from the cancel.

date.description: Date read from the cancel (ISO 8601 or partial text as transcribed).

cancel.description: Postmark or cancel detail when the item is used or the obliterator is salient.

centering_grade.description: Centering and margin balance for perforated issues, using the ladder common on eBay and in US expertizing (PSE, Philatelic Foundation): Gem/Superb through Fine/VF/XF slash grades (e.g. VF/XF, F-VF), then VG/Good/Average/Poor. MNH/NH/LH describe gum, not centering—use condition_quality for those.

image_basename.description: Image file name in uploads/ or media store.

x_min.description: Left edge of the box (0 = left, 1000 = right).

x_max.description: Right edge of the box (0 = left, 1000 = right).

y_min.description: Top edge of the box (0 = top, 1000 = bottom).

y_max.description: Bottom edge of the box (0 = top, 1000 = bottom).

bounding_box.description: Region of interest on the stamp or lot image; coordinates are permille (0–1000) along each axis relative to image width and height.

freeform_keywords.description: Ad-hoc keywords not yet mapped to thematic_tag.

stamp.description: Core record: country of issue, year, physical attributes (centering), condition, optional structured cancel or postmark when used, optional primary scott_catalog_entry (perforation and series context live on scott_catalog_entry when linked); additional catalog lines and thematic tags use child rows; optional pricing via listing.

catalog_line_id.description: Surrogate primary key for this catalog line.

catalog_system_abbreviation.description: Which catalog system the number belongs to (matches catalog_system primary key).

catalog_number.description: Number as printed in the catalog (e.g. 85a, 123 II, 4y).

supplement_notes.description: Specialized catalog notes, plate blocks, or variety suffixes.

stamp_catalog_number.description: Stores Scott, Michel, Facit, etc. numbers per stamp; a stamp may have multiple rows for different systems or supplements.

thematic_link_id.description: Surrogate primary key for this assignment.

role.description: How this tag relates to other tags on the same stamp.

confidence.description: Optional model or human confidence 0.0–1.0 for ML-assisted tagging.

notes.description: Rationale or caveats for this thematic assignment.

stamp_thematic_tag.description: Many-to-many link; supports primary vs secondary topical roles and free-text rationale.

tag_id.description: Stable slug for the tag (e.g. topical-ships, royalty-crowns).

name.description: Short label shown in filters and listings.

description.description: What designs or subjects belong under this tag.

thematic_tag.description: Normalized tags for ships, royalty, Olympics, etc.; optional parent builds a shallow hierarchy.

change.description: Change log entry describing a versioned modification to the consolidated data model or data.

git_commit_hash.description: Git commit hash corresponding to this change
